stretches.name: Neck Side Stretch
steps: Sit or stand tall with your shoulders relaxed.
Gently lower your right ear toward your right shoulder.
Hold, breathing slowly, feeling a stretch along the left side of your neck.
Return to center and switch sides.
stretches.breathingCue: Exhale slowly as you ease into the stretch.
commonMistakes: Don't lift your shoulder toward your ear.
Avoid forcing or bouncing the movement.
stretches.safetyNote: Move slowly and never stretch into sharp pain.

variants.gentle: Keep the range small and rest a hand in your lap.
variants.deeper: Let your hand gently rest on the side of your head for a touch more stretch.

stretches.name: Neck Rotation
steps: Sit tall with a long spine.
Slowly turn your head to look over your right shoulder.
Hold gently, keeping your shoulders still.
Return to center and turn to the left.
stretches.breathingCue: Breathe evenly throughout.
commonMistakes: Don't rush the rotation.
Keep your chin level, not tilted up.
stretches.safetyNote: If you feel dizzy, stop and return to center.

variants.gentle: Turn only as far as is comfortable.
variants.deeper: Pause and take one extra slow breath at the end of the turn.

stretches.name: Back-of-Neck Stretch
steps: Sit or stand tall with relaxed shoulders.
Gently drop your chin toward your chest.
Let the weight of your head create a stretch up the back of your neck.
Slowly lift your head back to neutral.
stretches.breathingCue: Exhale as your chin lowers.
commonMistakes: Don't round your upper back to reach further.
Avoid pulling your head down hard.
stretches.safetyNote: Keep the movement gentle; the neck is sensitive.

variants.gentle: Lower the chin only halfway.
variants.deeper: Rest your fingertips lightly on the back of your head.

stretches.name: Cross-Body Shoulder Stretch
steps: Bring your right arm straight across your chest.
Use your left hand to gently draw the arm closer.
Keep your shoulder down and relaxed.
Hold, then switch arms.
stretches.breathingCue: Exhale as you draw the arm in.
commonMistakes: Don't pull on the elbow joint itself.
Avoid shrugging the shoulder up.
stretches.safetyNote: Ease off if you feel pinching in the shoulder.

variants.gentle: Support the arm with less pressure.
variants.deeper: Draw the arm slightly closer to your chest.

stretches.name: Upper Back Rounding
steps: Clasp your hands together and extend your arms in front of you.
Round your upper back and push your hands away.
Let your head relax and feel the stretch between your shoulder blades.
Slowly return to upright.
stretches.breathingCue: Exhale as you round and reach forward.
commonMistakes: Don't hold your breath.
Keep the movement in your upper back, not your lower back.
stretches.safetyNote: Move smoothly in and out of the position.

variants.gentle: Reach forward only slightly.
variants.deeper: Tuck your chin and round a little more deeply.

stretches.name: Wall Shoulder Slide
steps: Stand facing a wall with forearms resting on it.
Keep your forearms in contact as you slide them slowly upward.
Stop where you feel a comfortable stretch through your shoulders.
Slide back down with control.
stretches.breathingCue: Breathe steadily as you slide upward.
commonMistakes: Don't arch your lower back.
Keep your shoulders away from your ears.
stretches.safetyNote: Stay within a pain-free range overhead.

variants.gentle: Slide up only to shoulder height.
variants.deeper: Reach a little higher while keeping ribs down.

stretches.name: Wrist Flexor Stretch
steps: Extend your right arm forward, palm up.
With your other hand, gently pull the fingers down and back.
Feel the stretch along the inside of your forearm.
Hold, then switch sides.
stretches.breathingCue: Exhale as you ease into the stretch.
commonMistakes: Don't lock the elbow harshly.
Keep the pull gentle.
stretches.safetyNote: Great for relief after typing; stop if you feel tingling.

variants.gentle: Bend the elbow slightly to reduce intensity.
variants.deeper: Straighten the arm fully for more stretch.

stretches.name: Wrist Extensor Stretch
steps: Extend your right arm forward, palm down.
Let your hand drop and gently pull it toward you.
Feel the stretch along the top of your forearm.
Hold, then switch sides.
stretches.breathingCue: Breathe slowly and relax the hand.
commonMistakes: Don't force the wrist.
Keep your shoulder relaxed.
stretches.safetyNote: Ease off if your wrist feels strained.

variants.gentle: Apply only light pressure.
variants.deeper: Straighten the elbow fully and curl the fingers under.

stretches.name: Overhead Triceps Stretch
steps: Raise your right arm overhead and bend the elbow so your hand drops behind your head.
Use your left hand to gently guide the elbow back.
Feel the stretch along the back of your upper arm.
Hold, then switch sides.
stretches.breathingCue: Exhale as you guide the elbow back.
commonMistakes: Don't push the neck forward.
Keep your ribs from flaring out.
stretches.safetyNote: Avoid if overhead positions bother your shoulder.

variants.gentle: Just lower the hand behind the head without assisting.
variants.deeper: Guide the elbow a little further back.

stretches.name: Doorway Chest Opener
steps: Stand in a doorway and place your forearms on the frame, elbows about shoulder height.
Step one foot through and lean gently forward.
Feel the stretch open across your chest.
Hold, then step back to release.
stretches.breathingCue: Take slow, full breaths to open the chest.
commonMistakes: Don't overarch your lower back.
Keep your chin level.
stretches.safetyNote: Lean only until you feel a comfortable stretch.

variants.gentle: Lean in just slightly.
variants.deeper: Step a little further through the doorway.

stretches.name: Behind-Back Chest Stretch
steps: Clasp your hands behind your lower back.
Gently straighten your arms and lift your hands slightly.
Draw your shoulder blades together and open your chest.
Release slowly.
stretches.breathingCue: Inhale to lift the chest, exhale to relax.
commonMistakes: Don't shrug the shoulders up.
Avoid forcing the arms high.
stretches.safetyNote: Keep the lift gentle if your shoulders are tight.

variants.gentle: Hold a towel between your hands if clasping is hard.
variants.deeper: Lift the hands a little higher while keeping ribs down.

stretches.name: Seated Spinal Twist
steps: Sit tall on a chair with feet flat on the floor.
Place your right hand on the chair back and left hand on your right knee.
Gently twist your torso to the right, lengthening your spine.
Hold, return to center, and twist to the left.
stretches.breathingCue: Inhale to lengthen, exhale to twist a little more.
commonMistakes: Don't yank yourself around with your arms.
Keep both hips on the seat.
stretches.safetyNote: Twist gently and stop at a comfortable point.

variants.gentle: Twist only a small amount.
variants.deeper: Use a slightly firmer hold to deepen the rotation.

stretches.name: Cat-Cow
steps: Come onto your hands and knees on a soft surface.
Inhale, drop your belly and lift your gaze (cow).
Exhale, round your back and tuck your chin (cat).
Flow slowly between the two for several breaths.
stretches.breathingCue: Inhale to arch, exhale to round.
commonMistakes: Don't rush the flow.
Keep your wrists under your shoulders.
stretches.safetyNote: Use a cushion under your knees if needed.

variants.gentle: Make the movements small and slow.
variants.deeper: Add a slightly larger arch and round each cycle.

stretches.name: Knees-to-Chest
steps: Lie on your back on a comfortable surface.
Draw both knees gently toward your chest.
Wrap your arms around your shins and relax your shoulders.
Hold, then lower your feet slowly.
stretches.breathingCue: Exhale as you hug the knees in.
commonMistakes: Don't lift your head and neck off the floor.
Keep the hold relaxed.
stretches.safetyNote: Gentle and soothing for the lower back.

variants.gentle: Hug one knee at a time.
variants.deeper: Gently rock side to side to massage the lower back.

stretches.name: Seated Figure-4
steps: Sit tall on a chair with feet flat.
Cross your right ankle over your left knee.
Keep your back straight and gently lean forward from the hips.
Hold, then switch sides.
stretches.breathingCue: Exhale as you hinge forward.
commonMistakes: Don't round your back to lean.
Keep the resting ankle flexed to protect the knee.
stretches.safetyNote: Lean only until you feel a comfortable glute stretch.

variants.gentle: Stay upright without leaning forward.
variants.deeper: Hinge a little further from the hips.

stretches.name: Standing Hip Flexor Stretch
steps: Step your right foot back into a small lunge.
Tuck your tailbone slightly and press your hips forward.
Feel the stretch at the front of your right hip.
Hold, then switch sides.
stretches.breathingCue: Exhale as you press the hips forward.
commonMistakes: Don't overarch your lower back.
Keep your front knee over your ankle.
stretches.safetyNote: Hold a wall or chair for balance if needed.

variants.gentle: Take a smaller step and press gently.
variants.deeper: Lengthen the stance and raise the same-side arm overhead.

stretches.name: Chair Hamstring Stretch
steps: Sit at the edge of a chair and extend your right leg forward, heel on the floor.
Keep your back straight and hinge forward slightly from the hips.
Feel the stretch along the back of your thigh.
Hold, then switch legs.
stretches.breathingCue: Exhale as you hinge forward.
commonMistakes: Don't round your back to reach.
Keep the extended knee soft, not locked.
stretches.safetyNote: A safe, supported option for tight hamstrings.

variants.gentle: Hinge just a little.
variants.deeper: Flex your foot and hinge slightly more.

stretches.name: Towel Hamstring Stretch
steps: Lie on your back and loop a towel around the sole of your right foot.
Straighten the leg upward, holding the towel ends.
Gently draw the leg toward you until you feel a stretch.
Hold, then switch legs.
stretches.breathingCue: Breathe slowly and relax into the stretch.
commonMistakes: Don't pull so hard your hips lift.
Keep your shoulders relaxed on the floor.
stretches.safetyNote: The towel lets you control the intensity precisely.

variants.gentle: Keep a slight bend in the knee.
variants.deeper: Straighten the knee and draw the leg a bit closer.

stretches.name: Standing Quad Stretch
steps: Stand near a wall and rest one hand on it for balance.
Bend your right knee and hold your right foot behind you.
Gently draw the heel toward your glutes, knees together.
Hold, then switch sides.
stretches.breathingCue: Breathe steadily and keep your hips level.
commonMistakes: Don't let the knee splay outward.
Avoid arching the lower back.
stretches.safetyNote: Always use the wall for balance to prevent falls.

variants.gentle: Hold a loop of towel around the ankle if reaching is hard.
variants.deeper: Tuck the tailbone to add a hip-flexor stretch.

stretches.name: Kneeling Hip Flexor Stretch
steps: Kneel on your right knee with your left foot flat in front (half-kneel).
Use a cushion under the kneeling knee for comfort.
Gently press your hips forward, tucking the tailbone.
Hold, then switch sides.
stretches.breathingCue: Exhale as you ease the hips forward.
commonMistakes: Don't let the front knee pass your toes.
Keep your chest tall.
stretches.safetyNote: Pad the knee; skip if kneeling is uncomfortable.

variants.gentle: Press forward only slightly.
variants.deeper: Raise the same-side arm overhead and lean gently.

stretches.name: Standing Calf Stretch
steps: Face a wall and place both hands on it at shoulder height.
Step your right foot back, heel pressing down, leg straight.
Lean toward the wall until you feel a calf stretch.
Hold, then switch legs.
stretches.breathingCue: Breathe slowly as you lean in.
commonMistakes: Don't let the back heel lift.
Keep the back leg straight for the upper calf.
stretches.safetyNote: Great pre- and post-walk; never bounce.

variants.gentle: Take a smaller step back.
variants.deeper: Bend the back knee slightly to reach the lower calf (soleus).

stretches.name: Ankle Circles
steps: Sit tall on a chair and lift your right foot off the floor.
Slowly circle the ankle in one direction.
Reverse the direction after a few circles.
Lower the foot and switch sides.
stretches.breathingCue: Breathe naturally and keep the motion smooth.
commonMistakes: Don't move the whole leg — isolate the ankle.
Keep the circles slow and controlled.
stretches.safetyNote: Gentle mobility, ideal for stiff or swollen ankles.

variants.gentle: Make smaller circles.
variants.deeper: Make larger circles and add gentle point-and-flex.

stretches.name: Chin Tuck
steps: Sit or stand tall, looking straight ahead.
Gently draw your chin straight back, making a 'double chin'.
Hold briefly, feeling a lengthening at the back of your neck.
Release and repeat slowly.
stretches.breathingCue: Exhale as you tuck the chin back.
commonMistakes: Don't tip your head down.
Keep the movement small and gentle.
stretches.safetyNote: A great posture reset; keep it pain-free.

variants.gentle: Do fewer, smaller repetitions.
variants.deeper: Hold each tuck a few seconds longer.

stretches.name: Seated Upper Trap Stretch
steps: Sit on a chair and hold the seat edge with your right hand.
Tilt your left ear toward your left shoulder.
Let your right shoulder anchor down for a deeper stretch.
Hold, then switch sides.
stretches.breathingCue: Breathe slowly and let the shoulder relax down.
commonMistakes: Don't pull hard on the head.
Keep the anchored shoulder down.
stretches.safetyNote: Anchoring the hand makes this effective — stay gentle.

variants.gentle: Skip the hand anchor and just tilt.
variants.deeper: Rest the opposite hand lightly on your head.

stretches.name: Shoulder Rolls
steps: Sit or stand tall with arms relaxed.
Roll both shoulders up, back, and down in a smooth circle.
Repeat several times, then reverse direction.
Keep the motion slow and easy.
stretches.breathingCue: Inhale as shoulders lift, exhale as they lower.
commonMistakes: Don't rush the circles.
Keep your neck relaxed.
stretches.safetyNote: A gentle warm-up; never force the range.

variants.gentle: Make the circles small.
variants.deeper: Make slow, full circles through the whole range.

stretches.name: Thread the Needle
steps: Start on your hands and knees on a soft surface.
Slide your right arm under your body, palm up, lowering your right shoulder.
Rest the side of your head down and feel the stretch across your upper back.
Return and switch sides.
stretches.breathingCue: Exhale as you thread the arm through.
commonMistakes: Don't collapse your neck — let it rest gently.
Keep your hips over your knees.
stretches.safetyNote: Use a cushion under the knees if needed.

variants.gentle: Reach only partway under.
variants.deeper: Extend the top arm overhead for more rotation.

stretches.name: Prayer Wrist Stretch
steps: Press your palms together in front of your chest, fingers pointing up.
Keeping palms together, slowly lower your hands toward your waist.
Stop where you feel a gentle stretch in your wrists and forearms.
Hold, then release.
stretches.breathingCue: Breathe slowly and keep your shoulders relaxed.
commonMistakes: Don't let the palms separate.
Lower only to a comfortable point.
stretches.safetyNote: Soothing after typing; keep it gentle.

variants.gentle: Lower the hands just slightly.
variants.deeper: Lower a little further while keeping palms flat together.

stretches.name: Wall Biceps Stretch
steps: Stand beside a wall and place your right palm flat on it behind you, arm straight.
Slowly turn your body away from the wall.
Feel the stretch along the front of your arm and shoulder.
Hold, then switch sides.
stretches.breathingCue: Exhale as you rotate away from the wall.
commonMistakes: Don't shrug the shoulder.
Turn gently — this is a sensitive area.
stretches.safetyNote: Ease off immediately if you feel any nerve tingling.

variants.gentle: Turn only slightly away.
variants.deeper: Rotate a bit further while keeping the arm straight.

stretches.name: Corner Chest Stretch
steps: Stand facing a wall corner with forearms on each wall, elbows shoulder height.
Step one foot forward and lean gently into the corner.
Feel your chest open across the front.
Hold, then step back.
stretches.breathingCue: Breathe deeply to expand the chest.
commonMistakes: Don't arch the lower back.
Keep elbows around shoulder height.
stretches.safetyNote: An easy, supported chest opener.

variants.gentle: Lean in just a little.
variants.deeper: Step further forward for a bigger stretch.

stretches.name: Lying Chest Opener
steps: Lie on your back and stretch both arms out to the sides like a 'T'.
Relax your shoulders toward the floor.
Let gravity gently open your chest.
Breathe and hold.
stretches.breathingCue: Take slow, expansive breaths.
commonMistakes: Don't force the arms down.
Keep your lower back relaxed.
stretches.safetyNote: Very gentle — ideal for tight, rounded shoulders.

variants.gentle: Place a rolled towel along your spine for support.
variants.deeper: Move the arms slightly higher into a 'Y' shape.

stretches.name: Seated Open-Arms Chest Stretch
steps: Sit tall on a chair, feet flat.
Reach both arms back and open them wide, palms forward.
Squeeze your shoulder blades together and lift your chest.
Hold, then relax.
stretches.breathingCue: Inhale to open, exhale to soften.
commonMistakes: Don't let your chin jut forward.
Keep shoulders down, away from ears.
stretches.safetyNote: Great quick reset between desk tasks.

variants.gentle: Open the arms only slightly.
variants.deeper: Reach the arms a little further back.

stretches.name: Child's Pose
steps: Kneel on a soft surface and sit your hips back toward your heels.
Walk your hands forward and lower your chest toward the floor.
Rest your forehead down and relax.
Breathe slowly and let your back lengthen.
stretches.breathingCue: Breathe into your back ribs.
commonMistakes: Don't hold tension in the shoulders.
Let the hips sink as comfort allows.
stretches.safetyNote: Place a cushion under hips or knees if needed.

variants.gentle: Widen the knees and rest on a pillow.
variants.deeper: Walk the hands further forward and to one side.

stretches.name: Standing Back Extension
steps: Stand tall with hands on your lower back.
Gently lean back, lifting your chest toward the ceiling.
Go only as far as is comfortable, supporting with your hands.
Return to upright and repeat.
stretches.breathingCue: Inhale as you gently extend back.
commonMistakes: Don't crank the neck back.
Keep the movement small and controlled.
stretches.safetyNote: A counter-stretch for sitting; avoid if it bothers your back.

variants.gentle: Lean back just slightly.
variants.deeper: Extend a touch further with full support.

stretches.name: Lying Figure-4
steps: Lie on your back with both knees bent.
Cross your right ankle over your left thigh.
Reach through and gently pull your left thigh toward your chest.
Hold, then switch sides.
stretches.breathingCue: Exhale as you draw the leg in.
commonMistakes: Don't lift your head and shoulders off the floor.
Keep the crossed foot flexed.
stretches.safetyNote: A comfortable, supported glute stretch.

variants.gentle: Keep the bottom foot on the floor instead of pulling in.
variants.deeper: Draw the thigh a little closer to your chest.

stretches.name: Butterfly Stretch
steps: Sit on the floor and bring the soles of your feet together.
Hold your ankles and let your knees relax outward.
Sit tall and hinge forward slightly from the hips.
Hold and breathe.
stretches.breathingCue: Exhale as you gently fold forward.
commonMistakes: Don't bounce the knees.
Keep your back long, not rounded.
stretches.safetyNote: Sit on a cushion if your back rounds.

variants.gentle: Move feet further from your body and stay upright.
variants.deeper: Bring feet closer and fold a little more.

stretches.name: Standing Glute Stretch
steps: Stand behind a chair and hold the back for balance.
Cross your right ankle over your left thigh, just above the knee.
Sit your hips back and down slightly, like a one-legged squat.
Hold, then switch sides.
stretches.breathingCue: Exhale as you sit your hips back.
commonMistakes: Don't round your back.
Keep the standing knee behind your toes.
stretches.safetyNote: Always hold the chair for steady balance.

variants.gentle: Sit back only a little.
variants.deeper: Sit the hips lower for a stronger stretch.

stretches.name: Standing Forward Fold
steps: Stand with feet hip-width apart, knees slightly bent.
Hinge at your hips and fold forward, letting your arms hang.
Relax your head and neck toward the floor.
Slowly roll back up to stand.
stretches.breathingCue: Exhale as you fold; breathe softly while holding.
commonMistakes: Don't lock your knees.
Come up slowly to avoid dizziness.
stretches.safetyNote: Keep a soft bend in the knees throughout.

variants.gentle: Bend the knees generously and rest hands on your shins.
variants.deeper: Straighten the legs more and let the hands reach the floor.

stretches.name: Table Hamstring Stretch
steps: Stand facing a sturdy table at about hip height.
Rest your right heel on the table with the leg straight.
Keep your back tall and hinge forward gently from the hips.
Hold, then switch legs.
stretches.breathingCue: Exhale as you hinge toward the leg.
commonMistakes: Don't round your back to reach.
Use a lower surface if the table is too high.
stretches.safetyNote: Use a stable, sturdy surface only.

variants.gentle: Use a lower surface and stay more upright.
variants.deeper: Flex your foot and hinge slightly further.

stretches.name: Lying Wall Hamstring Stretch
steps: Lie on your back near a doorway or wall corner.
Rest one heel up against the wall with the leg straight.
Let the other leg extend through the doorway or rest bent.
Relax and hold, then switch legs.
stretches.breathingCue: Breathe slowly and let the leg relax against the wall.
commonMistakes: Don't push into pain.
Keep your hips on the floor.
stretches.safetyNote: Very gentle and relaxing for tight hamstrings.

variants.gentle: Move further from the wall to ease the stretch.
variants.deeper: Scoot closer to the wall for more intensity.

stretches.name: Side-Lying Quad Stretch
steps: Lie on your left side with your head resting on your arm.
Bend your right knee and hold your right foot or ankle behind you.
Gently draw the heel toward your glutes, knees together.
Hold, then roll over and switch sides.
stretches.breathingCue: Breathe slowly and keep your hips stacked.
commonMistakes: Don't arch your lower back.
Keep the knees close together.
stretches.safetyNote: A stable, fall-free way to stretch the quad.

variants.gentle: Loop a towel around the ankle if reaching is hard.
variants.deeper: Gently press the hips forward as you hold the foot.

stretches.name: Kneeling Quad Stretch
steps: Kneel facing away from a wall, with a cushion under your knee.
Place the top of your right foot up against the wall behind you.
Bring your left foot forward into a half-kneel and rise tall.
Hold, then switch sides.
stretches.breathingCue: Exhale and keep your torso upright.
commonMistakes: Don't let the lower back overarch.
Keep the front knee over the ankle.
stretches.safetyNote: An intense stretch — skip if your knees are sensitive.

variants.gentle: Do the standing or side-lying quad stretch instead.
variants.deeper: Bring the front foot forward to lengthen the back-leg stretch.

stretches.name: Elevated Hip Flexor Stretch
steps: Stand a step in front of a chair, facing away from it.
Rest the top of your right foot back on the seat.
Bend your front knee and gently press your hips forward.
Hold, then switch sides.
stretches.breathingCue: Exhale as you press the hips forward.
commonMistakes: Don't overarch the lower back.
Keep the front knee over the ankle.
stretches.safetyNote: Hold a wall for balance; use a low, stable chair.

variants.gentle: Use the standing hip flexor stretch instead.
variants.deeper: Tuck the tailbone and raise the same-side arm overhead.

stretches.name: Step Calf Stretch
steps: Stand on a step or curb, holding a rail for balance.
Let your right heel drop below the step edge.
Feel the stretch lengthen through your calf.
Hold, then switch sides.
stretches.breathingCue: Breathe slowly as the heel lowers.
commonMistakes: Don't drop the heel suddenly.
Always hold a rail for safety.
stretches.safetyNote: Only do this with a secure handrail to prevent falls.

variants.gentle: Lower the heel only slightly.
variants.deeper: Let the heel sink a little further with control.

stretches.name: Seated Calf Stretch with Towel
steps: Sit on the floor with your right leg straight in front.
Loop a towel around the ball of your right foot.
Gently pull the towel to draw your toes toward you.
Hold, then switch legs.
stretches.breathingCue: Exhale as you draw the toes back.
commonMistakes: Don't pull so hard the knee bends.
Keep your back tall.
stretches.safetyNote: The towel gives you easy, precise control.

variants.gentle: Keep a small bend in the knee.
variants.deeper: Straighten the knee fully and pull a touch more.

stretches.name: Wall Ankle Stretch
steps: Stand facing a wall with your right foot a few inches away.
Keep your heel down and gently drive your knee toward the wall.
Feel the stretch deep in your ankle and lower calf.
Hold, then switch sides.
stretches.breathingCue: Breathe steadily as the knee moves forward.
commonMistakes: Don't let the heel lift.
Keep the knee tracking over the toes.
stretches.safetyNote: Great for ankle mobility before walking or squats.

variants.gentle: Place the foot closer to the wall.
variants.deeper: Move the foot slightly back for a larger range.
